stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 154.0 % and a standard deviation of 19.4 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.2% and 9.5%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 154.0 %. After correcting for the 6.8% bias: ≈ 144.2 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.2%-9.5%) and sampling variability: [130.6, 157.8]. Point estimate: 144.2 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
